<p dir="auto">Environment variables: <code>PORT</code> (default <code>3000</code>), <code>HOST</code> (default <code>0.0.0.0</code>).</p>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Note:</strong> Next.js standalone uses <code>HOSTNAME</code> for the bind address, but vinext uses <code>HOST</code> to avoid collision with the system-set <code>HOSTNAME</code> variable on Linux. Update your deployment config accordingly.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Starting a new vinext project</h3><a id="user-content-starting-a-new-vinext-project" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Starting a new vinext project" href="#starting-a-new-vinext-project"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Run <code>npm create next-app@latest</code> to create a new Next.js project, and then follow these instructions to migrate it to vinext.</p>
<p dir="auto">In the future, we will have a proper <code>npm create vinext</code> new project workflow.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Migrating an existing Next.js project</h3><a id="user-content-migrating-an-existing-nextjs-project" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Migrating an existing Next.js project" href="#migrating-an-existing-nextjs-project"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><code>vinext init</code> automates the migration in one command:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>npx vinext init</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="npx vinext init" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-check js-clipboard-check-icon color-fg-success d-none">

<p dir="auto">This will:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>Run <code>vinext check</code> to scan for compatibility issues</li>
<li>Install <code>vite</code>, <code>@vitejs/plugin-react</code>, and App Router-only deps (<code>@vitejs/plugin-rsc</code>, <code>react-server-dom-webpack</code>) as devDependencies</li>
<li>Rename CJS config files (e.g. <code>postcss.config.js</code> -> <code>.cjs</code>) to avoid ESM conflicts</li>
<li>Add <code>"type": "module"</code> to <code>package.json</code></li>
<li>Add <code>dev:vinext</code>, <code>build:vinext</code>, and <code>start:vinext</code> scripts to <code>package.json</code></li>
<li>Generate a minimal <code>vite.config.ts</code></li>
</ol>
<p dir="auto">The migration is non-destructive -- your existing Next.js setup continues to work alongside vinext. It does not modify <code>next.config</code>, <code>tsconfig.json</code>, or any source files, and it does not remove Next.js dependencies.</p>
<p dir="auto">vinext targets Vite 8, which defaults to Rolldown, Oxc, Lightning CSS, and a newer browser baseline. If you bring custom Vite config or plugins from an older setup, prefer <code>oxc</code>, <code>optimizeDeps.rolldownOptions</code>, and <code>build.rolldownOptions</code> over older <code>esbuild</code> and <code>build.rollupOptions</code> knobs, and override <code>build.target</code> if you still need older browsers. If a dependency breaks because of stricter CommonJS default import handling, fix the import or use <code>legacy.inconsistentCjsInterop: true</code> as a temporary escape hatch. See the <a href="https://vite.dev/guide/migration" rel="nofollow">Vite 8 migration guide</a>.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>npm run dev:vinext    <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Start the vinext dev server (port 3001)</span>
npm run build:vinext  <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Build production output with vinext</span>
npm run start:vinext  <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Start vinext production server</span>
npm run dev           <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Still runs Next.js as before</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="npm run dev:vinext    # Start the vinext dev server (port 3001)
npm run build:vinext  # Build production output with vinext
npm run start:vinext  # Start vinext production server
npm run dev           # Still runs Next.js as before" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

</svg>
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-check js-clipboard-check-icon color-fg-success d-none">

</svg>
    </clipboard-copy>

<p dir="auto">Use <code>--force</code> to overwrite an existing <code>vite.config.ts</code>, or <code>--skip-check</code> to skip the compatibility report.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Why</h2><a id="user-content-why" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Why" href="#why"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Vite has become the default build tool for modern web frameworks — fast HMR, a clean plugin API, native ESM, and a growing ecosystem. With <a href="https://github.com/vitejs/vite-plugin-react/tree/main/packages/plugin-rsc"><code>@vitejs/plugin-rsc</code></a> adding React Server Components support, it's now possible to build a full RSC framework on Vite.</p>
<p dir="auto">vinext is an experiment: can we reimplement the Next.js API surface on Vite, so that existing Next.js applications can run on a completely different toolchain? The answer, so far, is mostly yes.</p>
<p dir="auto">vinext works everywhere. It natively supports Cloudflare Workers (with <code>vinext deploy</code>, bindings, KV caching), and can be deployed to Vercel, Netlify, AWS, Deno Deploy, and more via the <a href="https://v3.nitro.build/" rel="nofollow">Nitro</a> Vite plugin. Native support for additional platforms is <a href="https://github.com/cloudflare/vinext/issues/80" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/cloudflare/vinext/issues/80/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">planned</a>.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Alternatives worth knowing about:</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong><a href="https://opennext.js.org/" rel="nofollow">OpenNext</a></strong> — adapts <code>next build</code> output for AWS, Cloudflare, and other platforms. OpenNext has been around much longer than vinext, is more mature, and covers more of the Next.js API surface because it builds on top of Next.js's own output rather than reimplementing it. If you want the safer, more proven option, start there.</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/deploying#self-hosting" rel="nofollow">Next.js self-hosting</a></strong> — Next.js can be deployed to any Node.js server, Docker container, or as a static export.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Design principles</h3><a id="user-content-design-principles" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Design principles" href="#design-principles"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Deploy anywhere.</strong> Natively supports Cloudflare Workers, with other platforms available via Nitro. Native adapters for more platforms are <a href="https://github.com/cloudflare/vinext/issues/80" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/cloudflare/vinext/issues/80/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">planned</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Pragmatic compatibility, not bug-for-bug parity.</strong> Targets 95%+ of real-world Next.js apps. Edge cases that depend on undocumented Vercel behavior are intentionally not supported.</li>
<li><strong>Latest Next.js only.</strong> Targets Next.js 16.x. No support for deprecated APIs from older versions.</li>
<li><strong>Incremental adoption.</strong> Drop in the plugin, fix what breaks, deploy.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">FAQ</h2><a id="user-content-faq" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: FAQ" href="#faq"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>What is this?</strong>
vinext is a Vite plugin that reimplements the public Next.js API — routing, server rendering, <code>next/*</code> module imports, the CLI — so you can run Next.js applications on Vite instead of the Next.js compiler toolchain. It can be deployed anywhere: Cloudflare Workers is the first natively supported target, with other platforms available via Nitro. Native adapters for more platforms are <a href="https://github.com/cloudflare/vinext/issues/80" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/cloudflare/vinext/issues/80/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">planned</a>.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Is this a fork of Next.js?</strong>
No. vinext is an alternative implementation of the Next.js API surface built on Vite. It does import some Next.js types and utilities, but the core is written from scratch. The goal is not to create a competing framework or add features beyond what Next.js offers — it's an experiment in how far AI-driven development and Vite's toolchain can go in replicating an existing, well-defined API surface.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>How is this different from OpenNext?</strong>
<a href="https://opennext.js.org/" rel="nofollow">OpenNext</a> adapts the <em>output</em> of a standard <code>next build</code> to run on various platforms. Because it builds on Next.js's own output, it inherits broad API coverage and has been well-tested for much longer. vinext takes a different approach: it reimplements the Next.js APIs on Vite from scratch, which means faster builds and smaller bundles, but less coverage of the long tail of Next.js features. If you need a mature, well-tested way to run Next.js outside Vercel, OpenNext is the safer choice. If you're interested in experimenting with a lighter toolchain and don't need every Next.js API, vinext might be worth a look.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Can I use this in production?</strong>
You can, with caution. This is experimental software with known bugs. It works well enough for demos and exploration, but it hasn't been battle-tested with real production traffic.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Can I just self-host Next.js?</strong>
Yes. Next.js supports <a href="https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/deploying#self-hosting" rel="nofollow">self-hosting</a> on Node.js servers, Docker containers, and static exports. If you're happy with the Next.js toolchain and just want to run it somewhere other than Vercel, self-hosting is the simplest path.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>How are you verifying this works?</strong>
The test suite has over 1,700 Vitest tests and 380 Playwright E2E tests. This includes tests ported directly from the <a href="https://github.com/vercel/next.js/tree/canary/test">Next.js test suite</a> and <a href="https://github.com/opennextjs/opennextjs-cloudflare">OpenNext's Cloudflare conformance suite</a>, covering routing, SSR, RSC, server actions, caching, metadata, middleware, streaming, and more. Vercel's <a href="https://github.com/vercel/next-app-router-playground">App Router Playground</a> also runs on vinext as an integration test. See the <a href="#tests">Tests</a> section and <code>tests/nextjs-compat/TRACKING.md</code> for details.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Who is reviewing this code?</strong>
A mix of humans and AI agents. Humans review PRs before they merge, focused on behavior, structure, and long-term direction. We lean heavily on agent-driven code review to catch issues at PR time and across the codebase. The test suite is the primary quality gate. Outside contributions and deeper human code review are very welcome.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Why Vite?</strong>
Vite is an excellent build tool with a rich plugin ecosystem, first-class ESM support, and fast HMR. The <a href="https://github.com/vitejs/vite-plugin-react/tree/main/packages/plugin-rsc"><code>@vitejs/plugin-rsc</code></a> plugin adds React Server Components support with multi-environment builds. This project is an experiment to see how much of the Next.js developer experience can be replicated on top of Vite's infrastructure.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Does this support the Pages Router, App Router, or both?</strong>
Both. File-system routing, SSR, client hydration, and deployment to Cloudflare Workers work for both routers.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>What version of Next.js does this target?</strong>
Next.js 16.x. No support for deprecated APIs from older versions.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Can I deploy to AWS/Netlify/other platforms?</strong>
Yes. Add the <a href="https://v3.nitro.build/" rel="nofollow">Nitro</a> Vite plugin alongside vinext, and you can deploy to Vercel, Netlify, AWS Amplify, Deno Deploy, Azure, and <a href="https://v3.nitro.build/deploy" rel="nofollow">many more</a>. See <a href="#other-platforms-via-nitro">Other platforms (via Nitro)</a> for setup. For Cloudflare Workers, the native integration (<code>vinext deploy</code>) gives you the smoothest experience. Native adapters for more platforms are <a href="https://github.com/cloudflare/vinext/issues/80" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/cloudflare/vinext/issues/80/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">planned</a>.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>What happens when Next.js releases a new feature?</strong>
We track the public Next.js API surface and add support for new stable features. Experimental or unstable Next.js features are lower priority. The plan is to add commit-level tracking of the Next.js repo so we can stay current as new versions are released.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Deployment</h2><a id="user-content-deployment" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Deployment" href="#deployment"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Cloudflare Workers</h3><a id="user-content-cloudflare-workers" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Cloudflare Workers" href="#cloudflare-workers"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">vinext has native integration with Cloudflare Workers through <code>@cloudflare/vite-plugin</code>, including bindings access via <code>cloudflare:workers</code>, KV caching, image optimization, and the <code>vinext deploy</code> one-command workflow.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Prerequisites</h4><a id="user-content-prerequisites" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Prerequisites" href="#prerequisites"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Before running <code>vinext deploy</code> for the first time you need to authenticate with Cloudflare and tell wrangler which account to deploy to.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Authentication — pick one:</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong><code>wrangler login</code></strong> (recommended for local development) — opens a browser window to authenticate. Run it once and wrangler caches the token.</li>
<li><strong><code>CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN</code> env var</strong> (CI / non-interactive) — create a token at <a href="https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens" rel="nofollow">dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens</a> using the <strong>Edit Cloudflare Workers</strong> template. That template grants all the permissions <code>vinext deploy</code> needs.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Account ID:</strong></p>
<p dir="auto">wrangler needs to know which Cloudflare account to deploy to. Add your account ID to <code>wrangler.jsonc</code>:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-json-comments notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>{
  <span class="pl-ent">"account_id"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span><your-account-id><span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
  <span class="pl-ii">...</span>
}</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

  "account_id": "<your-account-id>",
  ...
}" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">Find your account ID in the Cloudflare dashboard URL (<code>dash.cloudflare.com/<account-id></code>) or by running <code>wrangler whoami</code> after logging in.</p>
<p dir="auto">Alternatively, set the <code>CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID</code> environment variable instead of hardcoding it in the config file.</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>vinext deploy</code> auto-generates the necessary configuration files (<code>vite.config.ts</code>, <code>wrangler.jsonc</code>, <code>worker/index.ts</code>) if they don't exist, builds the application, and deploys to Workers.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>vinext deploy
vinext deploy --env staging</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">Use <code>--env <name></code> to target <code>wrangler.jsonc</code> <code>env.<name></code>. <code>--preview</code> is shorthand for <code>--env preview</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">The deploy command also auto-detects and fixes common migration issues:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Adds <code>"type": "module"</code> to package.json if missing</li>
<li>Resolves tsconfig.json path aliases automatically (via <code>vite-tsconfig-paths</code>)</li>
<li>Detects MDX usage and configures <code>@mdx-js/rollup</code></li>
<li>Renames CJS config files (postcss.config.js, etc.) to <code>.cjs</code> when needed</li>
<li>Detects native Node.js modules (sharp, resvg, satori, lightningcss, @napi-rs/canvas) and auto-stubs them for Workers. If you encounter others that need stubbing, PRs are welcome.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Both App Router and Pages Router work on Workers with full client-side hydration.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Cloudflare Bindings (D1, R2, KV, AI, etc.)</h4><a id="user-content-cloudflare-bindings-d1-r2-kv-ai-etc" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Cloudflare Bindings (D1, R2, KV, AI, etc.)" href="#cloudflare-bindings-d1-r2-kv-ai-etc"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Use <code>import { env } from "cloudflare:workers"</code> to access bindings in any server component, route handler, or server action. No custom worker entry or special configuration required.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-tsx notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span> <span class="pl-s1">env</span> <span class="pl-kos">}</span> <span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s">"cloudflare:workers"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

<span class="pl-k">export</span> <span class="pl-k">default</span> <span class="pl-k">async</span> <span class="pl-k">function</span> <span class="pl-v">Page</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
  <span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">result</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-k">await</span> <span class="pl-s1">env</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">DB</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">prepare</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s">"SELECT * FROM posts"</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">all</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
  <span class="pl-k">return</span> <span class="pl-c1"><</span><span class="pl-s1">div</span><span class="pl-c1">></span><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-c1">JSON</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">stringify</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">result</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos"></</span><span class="pl-s1">div</span><span class="pl-c1">></span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-kos">}</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="import { env } from "cloudflare:workers";

export default async function Page() {
  const result = await env.DB.prepare("SELECT * FROM posts").all();
  return <div>{JSON.stringify(result)}</div>;
}" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">This works because <code>@cloudflare/vite-plugin</code> runs the RSC environment in workerd, where <code>cloudflare:workers</code> is a native module. In production builds, the import is externalized so workerd resolves it at runtime. All binding types are supported: D1, R2, KV, Durable Objects, AI, Queues, Vectorize, Browser Rendering, etc.</p>
<p dir="auto">Define your bindings in <code>wrangler.jsonc</code> as usual:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-json-comments notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>{
  <span class="pl-ent">"name"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>my-app<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
  <span class="pl-ent">"compatibility_date"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>2026-02-12<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
  <span class="pl-ent">"compatibility_flags"</span>: [<span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>nodejs_compat<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>],
  <span class="pl-ent">"d1_databases"</span>: [{ <span class="pl-ent">"binding"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>DB<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>, <span class="pl-ent">"database_name"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>my-db<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>, <span class="pl-ent">"database_id"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>...<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span> }],
  <span class="pl-ent">"kv_namespaces"</span>: [{ <span class="pl-ent">"binding"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>CACHE<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>, <span class="pl-ent">"id"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>...<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span> }],
}</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="{
  "name": "my-app",
  "compatibility_date": "2026-02-12",
  "compatibility_flags": ["nodejs_compat"],
  "d1_databases": [{ "binding": "DB", "database_name": "my-db", "database_id": "..." }],
  "kv_namespaces": [{ "binding": "CACHE", "id": "..." }],
}" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">For TypeScript types, generate them with <code>wrangler types</code> and the <code>env</code> import will be fully typed.</p>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Note:</strong> You do not need <code>getPlatformProxy()</code>, a custom worker entry with <code>fetch(request, env)</code>, or any other workaround. <code>cloudflare:workers</code> is the recommended way to access bindings in vinext.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Traffic-aware Pre-Rendering (experimental)</h4><a id="user-content-traffic-aware-pre-rendering-experimental" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Traffic-aware Pre-Rendering (experimental)" href="#traffic-aware-pre-rendering-experimental"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">TPR queries Cloudflare zone analytics at deploy time to find which pages actually get traffic, pre-renders only those, and uploads them to KV cache. The result is SSG-level latency for popular pages without pre-rendering your entire site.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>vinext deploy --experimental-tpr                    <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Pre-render pages covering 90% of traffic</span>
vinext deploy --experimental-tpr --tpr-coverage 95  <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> More aggressive coverage</span>
vinext deploy --experimental-tpr --tpr-limit 500    <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Cap at 500 pages</span>
vinext deploy --experimental-tpr --tpr-window 48    <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Use 48h of analytics</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="vinext deploy --experimental-tpr                    # Pre-render pages covering 90% of traffic
vinext deploy --experimental-tpr --tpr-coverage 95  # More aggressive coverage
vinext deploy --experimental-tpr --tpr-limit 500    # Cap at 500 pages
vinext deploy --experimental-tpr --tpr-window 48    # Use 48h of analytics" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">
    <path d="M0 6.75C0 5.784.784 5 1.75 5h1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-1.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0v1.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 9.25 16h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 14.25Z"></path><path d="M5 1.75C5 .784 5.784 0 6.75 0h7.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 11h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 5 9.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-7.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Z"></path>
</svg>
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-check js-clipboard-check-icon color-fg-success d-none">
    <path d="M13.78 4.22a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.06l-7.25 7.25a.75.75 0 0 1-1.06 0L2.22 9.28a.751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018L6 10.94l6.72-6.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0Z"></path>
</svg>
    </clipboard-copy>
  </div></div>
<p dir="auto">Requires a custom domain (zone analytics are unavailable on <code>*.workers.dev</code>) and <code>CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN</code> with Zone.Analytics read permission.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Custom Vite configuration</h4><a id="user-content-custom-vite-configuration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Custom Vite configuration" href="#custom-vite-configuration"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">If you need to customize the Vite config, create a <code>vite.config.ts</code>. vinext will merge its config with yours. This is required for Cloudflare Workers deployment with the App Router (RSC needs explicit plugin configuration):</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ts notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span> <span class="pl-s1">defineConfig</span> <span class="pl-kos">}</span> <span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s">"vite"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">vinext</span> <span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s">"vinext"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">rsc</span> <span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s">"@vitejs/plugin-rsc"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span> <span class="pl-s1">cloudflare</span> <span class="pl-kos">}</span> <span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s">"@cloudflare/vite-plugin"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

<span class="pl-k">export</span> <span class="pl-k">default</span> <span class="pl-en">defineConfig</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">{</span>
  <span class="pl-c1">plugins</span>: <span class="pl-kos">[</span>
    <span class="pl-en">vinext</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
    <span class="pl-en">rsc</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">{</span>
      <span class="pl-c1">entries</span>: <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
        <span class="pl-c1">rsc</span>: <span class="pl-s">"virtual:vinext-rsc-entry"</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
        <span class="pl-c1">ssr</span>: <span class="pl-s">"virtual:vinext-app-ssr-entry"</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
        <span class="pl-c1">client</span>: <span class="pl-s">"virtual:vinext-app-browser-entry"</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
      <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
    <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
    <span class="pl-en">cloudflare</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">{</span>
      <span class="pl-c1">viteEnvironment</span>: <span class="pl-kos">{</span> <span class="pl-c1">name</span>: <span class="pl-s">"rsc"</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-c1">childEnvironments</span>: <span class="pl-kos">[</span><span class="pl-s">"ssr"</span><span class="pl-kos">]</span> <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
    <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
  <span class="pl-kos">]</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
<span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="import { defineConfig } from "vite";
import vinext from "vinext";
import rsc from "@vitejs/plugin-rsc";
import { cloudflare } from "@cloudflare/vite-plugin";

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    vinext(),
    rsc({
      entries: {
        rsc: "virtual:vinext-rsc-entry",
        ssr: "virtual:vinext-app-ssr-entry",
        client: "virtual:vinext-app-browser-entry",
      },
    }),
    cloudflare({
      viteEnvironment: { name: "rsc", childEnvironments: ["ssr"] },
    }),
  ],
});" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

<p dir="auto">See the <a href="#live-examples">examples</a> for complete working configurations.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Other platforms (via Nitro)</h3><a id="user-content-other-platforms-via-nitro" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Other platforms (via Nitro)" href="#other-platforms-via-nitro"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">For deploying to platforms other than Cloudflare, vinext works with <a href="https://v3.nitro.build/" rel="nofollow">Nitro</a> as a Vite plugin. Add <code>nitro</code> alongside <code>vinext</code> in your Vite config and deploy to any <a href="https://v3.nitro.build/deploy" rel="nofollow">Nitro-supported platform</a>.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ts notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span> <span class="pl-s1">defineConfig</span> <span class="pl-kos">}</span> <span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s">"vite"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">vinext</span> <span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s">"vinext"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span> <span class="pl-s1">nitro</span> <span class="pl-kos">}</span> <span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s">"nitro/vite"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

<span class="pl-k">export</span> <span class="pl-k">default</span> <span class="pl-en">defineConfig</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">{</span>
  <span class="pl-c1">plugins</span>: <span class="pl-kos">[</span><span class="pl-en">vinext</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-en">nitro</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">]</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
<span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="import { defineConfig } from "vite";
import vinext from "vinext";
import { nitro } from "nitro/vite";

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [vinext(), nitro()],
});" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>npm install nitro</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">Nitro auto-detects the deployment platform in most CI/CD environments (Vercel, Netlify, AWS Amplify, Azure, and others), so you typically don't need to set a preset. For local builds, set the <code>NITRO_PRESET</code> environment variable:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>NITRO_PRESET=vercel npx vite build
NITRO_PRESET=netlify npx vite build
NITRO_PRESET=deno_deploy npx vite build</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="NITRO_PRESET=vercel npx vite build

<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Deploying to Cloudflare?</strong> You can use Nitro, but the native integration (<code>vinext deploy</code> / <code>@cloudflare/vite-plugin</code>) is recommended. It provides the best developer experience with <code>cloudflare:workers</code> bindings, KV caching, image optimization, and one-command deploys.</p>
</blockquote>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">API coverage</h2><a id="user-content-api-coverage" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: API coverage" href="#api-coverage"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">~94% of the Next.js 16 API surface has full or partial support. The remaining gaps are intentional stubs for deprecated features and Partial Prerendering (which Next.js 16 reworked into <code>"use cache"</code> — that directive is fully supported).</p>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">✅ = full implementation | 🟡 = partial (runtime behavior correct, some build-time optimizations missing) | ⬜ = intentional stub/no-op</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Module shims</h3><a id="user-content-module-shims" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Module shims" href="#module-shims"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Every <code>next/*</code> import is shimmed to a Vite-compatible implementation.</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Module</th>
<th></th>
<th>Notes</th>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Environment variable loading (<code>.env*</code>)</h3><a id="user-content-environment-variable-loading-env" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Environment variable loading (.env*)" href="#environment-variable-loading-env"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">vinext automatically loads dotenv files for <code>dev</code>, <code>build</code>, <code>start</code>, and <code>deploy</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">Load order matches Next.js (highest priority first):</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>Existing <code>process.env</code> values (shell/CI)</li>
<li><code>.env.<mode>.local</code></li>
<li><code>.env.local</code> (skipped when mode is <code>test</code>)</li>
<li><code>.env.<mode></code></li>
<li><code>.env</code></li>
</ol>
<p dir="auto">Modes:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>vinext dev</code> uses <code>development</code></li>
<li><code>vinext build</code>, <code>vinext start</code>, and <code>vinext deploy</code> use <code>production</code></li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Variable expansion (<code>$VAR</code> / <code>${VAR}</code>) is supported.</p>
<p dir="auto">Client exposure remains explicit:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>NEXT_PUBLIC_*</code> variables are inlined for browser usage</li>
<li><code>next.config.js</code> <code>env</code> entries are also inlined</li>
<li>Other env vars stay server-only unless you explicitly expose them through Vite (for example <code>VITE_*</code> + <code>import.meta.env</code>)</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Override behavior:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>To override any <code>.env*</code> value, set it in your shell/CI environment before running vinext. Existing <code>process.env</code> always wins.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Caching</h3><a id="user-content-caching" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Caching" href="#caching"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The cache is pluggable. The default <code>MemoryCacheHandler</code> works out of the box. Swap in your own backend for production.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Configuring cache adapters from <code>vite.config</code></h4><a id="user-content-configuring-cache-adapters-from-viteconfig" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Configuring cache adapters from vite.config" href="#configuring-cache-adapters-from-viteconfig"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Instead of wiring up cache handlers imperatively from a worker entry, you can declare them in the <code>vinext()</code> plugin config. The <code>@vinext/cloudflare</code> package ships two Cloudflare adapters for this:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong><code>kvDataAdapter()</code></strong> (<code>@vinext/cloudflare/cache/kv-data-adapter</code>) — backs the <code>"use cache"</code> data cache with a Workers KV namespace.</li>
<li><strong><code>cdnAdapter()</code></strong> (<code>@vinext/cloudflare/cache/cdn-adapter</code>) — backs full-route CDN caching with the Workers Cache API.</li>
</ul>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ts notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span> <span class="pl-s1">defineConfig</span> <span class="pl-kos">}</span> <span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s">"vite"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">vinext</span> <span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s">"vinext"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span> <span class="pl-s1">cdnAdapter</span> <span class="pl-kos">}</span> <span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s">"@vinext/cloudflare/cache/cdn-adapter"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span> <span class="pl-s1">kvDataAdapter</span> <span class="pl-kos">}</span> <span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s">"@vinext/cloudflare/cache/kv-data-adapter"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

<span class="pl-k">export</span> <span class="pl-k">default</span> <span class="pl-en">defineConfig</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">{</span>
  <span class="pl-c1">plugins</span>: <span class="pl-kos">[</span>
    <span class="pl-en">vinext</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">{</span>
      <span class="pl-c1">cache</span>: <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
        <span class="pl-c1">cdn</span>: <span class="pl-en">cdnAdapter</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
        <span class="pl-c1">data</span>: <span class="pl-en">kvDataAdapter</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
      <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
    <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
  <span class="pl-kos">]</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
<span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">The KV data adapter reads <code>env[binding]</code> at runtime, so add the matching KV namespace to your <code>wrangler.jsonc</code>:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-json-comments notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>{
  <span class="pl-ent">"kv_namespaces"</span>: [{ <span class="pl-ent">"binding"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>VINEXT_KV_CACHE<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>, <span class="pl-ent">"id"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span><your-namespace-id><span class="pl-pds">"</span></span> }],
}</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="{
  "kv_namespaces": [{ "binding": "VINEXT_KV_CACHE", "id": "<your-namespace-id>" }],
}" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto"><code>binding</code> defaults to <code>VINEXT_KV_CACHE</code>, so <code>kvDataAdapter()</code> with no options works as long as that's your binding name. Other options: <code>appPrefix</code> (namespace cache keys to isolate multiple apps in one KV namespace), <code>ttlSeconds</code> (default KV <code>expirationTtl</code>, default 30 days), and <code>tagCacheTtlMs</code> (in-memory tag-invalidation cache TTL, default 5s). <code>cdnAdapter()</code> takes no options — it just needs the Workers Cache, which is always available on Workers.</p>
<p dir="auto">Each builder returns a plain, serializable <code>{ adapter, options }</code> descriptor — <strong>it never touches the Workers runtime</strong>, so nothing throws at build or dev time when bindings aren't available. The actual adapter (and its <code>env</code> binding lookup) is instantiated lazily on the first request.</p>
<p dir="auto">Registration is wired into <strong>every router and runtime</strong> — App Router and Pages Router, on Cloudflare Workers as well as the Node.js server (<code>vinext start</code>) and dev. It self-guards (instantiated once per isolate) and is resilient: if an adapter can't initialize on a given runtime (e.g. a KV binding doesn't exist on the Node server), vinext logs a warning and falls back to the default handler instead of failing requests.</p>
<p dir="auto">To write your own adapter, point a slot at any module by path and default-export a factory that receives <code>{ env, options }</code> at runtime and returns a data-cache <code>CacheHandler</code> (or a CDN adapter):</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ts notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-en">vinext</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">{</span>
  <span class="pl-c1">cache</span>: <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
    <span class="pl-c1">data</span>: <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
      <span class="pl-c1">adapter</span>: <span class="pl-en">require</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">resolve</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s">"./my-adapter.js"</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
      <span class="pl-c1">options</span>: <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
        <span class="pl-c">/* … */</span>
      <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
    <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
  <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
<span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What's NOT supported (and won't be)</h2><a id="user-content-whats-not-supported-and-wont-be" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What's NOT supported (and won't be)" href="#whats-not-supported-and-wont-be"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">These are intentional exclusions. For things that are missing today but on the roadmap, see <a href="#known-limitations">Known limitations</a> below.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Vercel-specific features</strong> — <code>@vercel/og</code> edge runtime, Vercel Analytics integration, Vercel KV/Blob/Postgres bindings. Use platform equivalents.</li>
<li><strong>AMP</strong> — Deprecated since Next.js 13. <code>useAmp()</code> returns <code>false</code>.</li>
<li><strong><code>next export</code> (legacy)</strong> — Use <code>output: 'export'</code> in config instead.</li>
<li><strong>Turbopack/webpack configuration</strong> — This runs on Vite. Use Vite plugins instead of webpack loaders/plugins.</li>
<li><strong><code>next/jest</code></strong> — Use Vitest.</li>
<li><strong><code>create-next-app</code> scaffolding</strong> — Not a goal.</li>
<li><strong>Bug-for-bug parity with undocumented behavior</strong> — If it's not in the Next.js docs, we probably don't replicate it.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Known limitations</h2><a id="user-content-known-limitations" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Known limitations" href="#known-limitations"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">These are gaps we'd like to close — distinct from the <a href="#whats-not-supported-and-wont-be">intentional exclusions</a> above.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Image optimization doesn't happen at build time.</strong> Remote images work via <code>@unpic/react</code> (auto-detects 28 CDN providers). Local images are routed through a <code>/_next/image</code> endpoint that can resize and transcode on Cloudflare Workers (via the Images binding) in production, but no build-time optimization or static resizing occurs.</li>
<li><strong>Google Fonts are loaded from the CDN, not self-hosted.</strong> No <code>size-adjust</code> fallback font metrics. Local fonts work but <code>@font-face</code> CSS is injected at runtime, not extracted at build time.</li>
<li><strong>Route segment config</strong> — <code>runtime</code> and <code>preferredRegion</code> are ignored (everything runs in the same environment).</li>
<li><strong>Node.js production server (<code>vinext start</code>)</strong> works for testing but is less complete than Workers deployment. Cloudflare Workers is the primary target.</li>
<li><strong>Native Node modules (sharp, resvg, satori, lightningcss, @napi-rs/canvas)</strong> crash Vite's RSC dev environment. Dynamic OG image/icon routes using these work in production builds but not in dev mode. These are auto-stubbed during <code>vinext deploy</code>.</li>
<li><strong><code>next.config.ts</code> <code>baseUrl</code> bare imports require Vite 8.</strong> A <code>next.config.ts</code> that imports a bare specifier resolved through <code>tsconfig.json</code>'s <code>compilerOptions.baseUrl</code> (e.g. <code>import { bar } from "bar"</code> resolving to a local <code>bar.ts</code>) relies on Vite 8's native <code>resolve.tsconfigPaths</code> (Rolldown/oxc-resolver). On Vite 7 there is no native equivalent, so these imports are not resolved. <code>compilerOptions.paths</code> aliases (e.g. <code>@/foo</code>) work on both Vite 7 and 8. Note that if a bare import matches both a <code>baseUrl</code>-local file and an installed package of the same name, the installed package wins (vinext keeps packages externalized so CJS config plugins like <code>@next/mdx</code> keep working).</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Benchmarks</h2><a id="user-content-benchmarks" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Benchmarks" href="#benchmarks"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Caveat:</strong> Benchmarks are hard to get right and these are early results. Take them as directional, not definitive.</p>
</blockquote>
<p dir="auto">These benchmarks measure <strong>compilation and bundling speed</strong>, not production serving performance. Next.js and vinext have fundamentally different default approaches: Next.js statically pre-renders pages at build time (making builds slower but production serving faster for static content), while vinext server-renders all pages on each request. To make the comparison apples-to-apples, the benchmark app uses <code>export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"</code> to disable Next.js static pre-rendering — both frameworks are doing the same work: compiling, bundling, and preparing server-rendered routes.</p>
<p dir="auto">The benchmark app is a shared 33-route App Router application (server components, client components, dynamic routes, nested layouts, API routes) built identically by both tools. We compare Next.js (Turbopack) against vinext (Vite 8). Both Turbopack and Rolldown parallelize across cores, so results on machines with more cores may differ significantly.</p>
<p dir="auto">We measure three things:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Production build time</strong> — 5 runs, timed with <code>hyperfine</code>.</li>
<li><strong>Client bundle size</strong> — gzipped output of each build.</li>
<li><strong>Dev server cold start</strong> — 10 runs, randomized execution order. Vite's dependency optimizer cache is cleared before each run.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Benchmarks run on GitHub CI runners (2-core Ubuntu) on every merge to <code>main</code>. See the launch numbers in the <a href="https://blog.cloudflare.com/vinext/" rel="nofollow">announcement blog post</a> and the latest results at <strong><a href="https://benchmarks.vinext.workers.dev" rel="nofollow">benchmarks.vinext.workers.dev</a></strong>.</p>
<details>
<summary>Why the bundle size difference?</summary>
<p dir="auto">Analysis of the build output shows two main factors:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li><strong>Tree-shaking</strong>: Vite/Rollup produces a smaller React+ReactDOM bundle than Next.js/Turbopack. Rollup's more aggressive dead-code elimination accounts for roughly half the overall difference.</li>
<li><strong>Framework overhead</strong>: Next.js ships more client-side infrastructure (router, Turbopack runtime loader, prefetching, error handling) than vinext's lighter client runtime.</li>
</ol>
<p dir="auto">Both frameworks ship the same app code and the same RSC client runtime (<code>react-server-dom-webpack</code>). The difference is in how much of React's internals survive tree-shaking and how much framework plumbing each tool adds.</p>
</details>
<p dir="auto">Reproduce with <code>node benchmarks/run.mjs --runs=5 --dev-runs=10</code>. Exact framework versions are recorded in each result.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Architecture</h2><a id="user-content-architecture" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Architecture" href="#architecture"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">vinext is a Vite plugin that:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li><strong>Resolves all <code>next/*</code> imports</strong> to local shim modules that reimplement the Next.js API using standard Web APIs and React primitives.</li>
<li><strong>Scans your <code>pages/</code> and <code>app/</code> directories</strong> to build a file-system router matching Next.js conventions.</li>
<li><strong>Generates virtual entry modules</strong> for the RSC, SSR, and browser environments that handle request routing, component rendering, and client hydration.</li>
<li><strong>Integrates with <code>@vitejs/plugin-rsc</code></strong> for React Server Components — handling <code>"use client"</code> / <code>"use server"</code> directives, RSC stream serialization, and multi-environment builds.</li>
</ol>
<p dir="auto">The result is a standard Vite application that happens to be API-compatible with Next.js.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Pages Router flow</h3><a id="user-content-pages-router-flow" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Pages Router flow" href="#pages-router-flow"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>Request → Vite dev server middleware → Route match → getServerSideProps/getStaticProps
  → renderToReadableStream(App + Page) → HTML with __NEXT_DATA__ → Client hydration
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

      pages-client-entry.ts # Pages Router client entry generator
    server/
      dev-server.ts       # Pages Router SSR request handler

      metadata-routes.ts  # File-based metadata route scanner

examples/                 # Deployed demo apps (see Live Examples above)

<p dir="auto">E2E tests cover Pages Router (dev + production), App Router (dev), and both routers on Cloudflare Workers via <code>wrangler dev</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">The <a href="https://github.com/vercel/next-app-router-playground">Vercel App Router Playground</a> runs on vinext as an integration test — see it live at <a href="https://app-router-playground.vinext.workers.dev" rel="nofollow">app-router-playground.vinext.workers.dev</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Local setup (from source)</h2><a id="user-content-local-setup-from-source" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Local setup (from source)" href="#local-setup-from-source"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>git clone https://github.com/cloudflare/vinext.git
<span class="pl-c1">cd</span> vinext
pnpm install
pnpm run build</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

cd vinext
pnpm install
pnpm run build" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">This builds the vinext package to <code>packages/vinext/dist/</code>. For active development, use <code>pnpm --filter vinext run dev</code> to rebuild on changes.</p>
<p dir="auto">To use it against an external Next.js app, link the built package:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> From your Next.js project directory:</span>
pnpm link /path/to/vinext/packages/vinext</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

pnpm link /path/to/vinext/packages/vinext" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">Or add it to your <code>package.json</code> as a file dependency:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-json notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>{
  <span class="pl-ent">"dependencies"</span>: {
    <span class="pl-ent">"vinext"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>file:/path/to/vinext/packages/vinext<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
  }
}</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">vinext has peer dependencies on <code>react ^19.2.6</code>, <code>react-dom ^19.2.6</code>, <code>react-server-dom-webpack ^19.2.6</code>, and <code>vite ^7.0.0 || ^8.0.0</code>. Then replace <code>next</code> with <code>vinext</code> in your scripts and run as normal.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Contributing</h2><a id="user-content-contributing" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Contributing" href="#contributing"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">This project is experimental and under active development. Issues and PRs are welcome.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">CI</h3><a id="user-content-ci" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: CI" href="#ci"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">When you open a PR, CI (check, Vitest, Playwright E2E) runs automatically. First-time contributors need one manual approval from a maintainer, then subsequent PRs run without intervention.</p>
<p dir="auto">Deploy previews (building and deploying examples to Cloudflare Workers) only run for branches pushed to the main repo. If you're a Cloudflare employee, push your branch to the main repo instead of forking, and previews deploy automatically. For fork PRs, a maintainer can comment <code>/deploy-preview</code> to trigger the deploy and post preview URLs.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Reporting bugs</h3><a id="user-content-reporting-bugs" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Reporting bugs" href="#reporting-bugs"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">If something doesn't work with your Next.js app, please file an issue — we want to hear about it.</p>
<p dir="auto">Before you do, try pointing an AI agent at the problem. Open your project with Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, or whatever you use, and ask it to figure out why your app isn't working with vinext. In our experience, agents are very good at tracing through the vinext source, identifying the gap or bug, and often producing a fix or at least a clear diagnosis. An issue that includes "here's what the agent found" is significantly more actionable than "it doesn't work."</p>
<p dir="auto">Even a partial diagnosis helps — stack traces, which <code>next/*</code> import is involved, whether it's a dev or production build issue, App Router vs Pages Router. The more context, the faster we can fix it.</p>
